skill.name: next-upgrade
skill.description: Upgrade Next.js to the latest version following official migration guides and codemods
skill.summary: Upgrade Next.js to the latest version following official migration guides and codemods
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static command and network findings are partly confirmed: the skill instructs agents to fetch official Next.js documentation and run npm or npx upgrade commands in a user project. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives; no cryptographic API, weak algorithm choice, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Package Upgrade Commands Require User Approval
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run npx codemods and npm install commands. This is expected for a Next.js upgrade workflow, but it can execute third-party package code and modify project dependencies.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and operational, and package-manager execution can change project state. The context is legitimate migration work, so this is moderate risk rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Fetching
low_findings.description: The skill asks agents to fetch Next.js upgrade documentation from nextjs.org. This network access is relevant to the task and points to official documentation, but it should still be visible to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit and all point to nextjs.org documentation. No evidence of credential transfer, tracking endpoints, or unrelated domains was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines are front matter, prose, or upgrade instructions, with no hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows no cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive algorithm. The matches appear to be pattern noise from upgrade-related text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution Via npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The codemod step uses npx with the latest version of @next/codemod. This is common for framework migrations, but it executes package code downloaded or resolved by the package manager.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is directly present in the skill. The risk is inherent to executing package-manager tooling, even though the package is official Next.js tooling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dependency Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to install latest framework, React, and type packages. This changes package manifests and lockfiles and may introduce breaking changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npm install commands are explicit and affect dependency state. The instructions are appropriate for an upgrade skill, but they require review before execution.

content.value_statement: Next.js upgrades often require several migration steps, codemods, and dependency changes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through official upgrade paths and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Checks package.json to identify the current Next.js and React versions.
Directs the agent to fetch official Next.js codemod and version upgrade guides.
Plans incremental major-version upgrades when projects span multiple releases.
Recommends official Next.js codemods for known breaking changes.
Updates framework, React, React DOM, and type dependencies together.
Prompts build and development server checks after the upgrade.
limitations: It does not replace manual review of breaking changes.
It depends on the current official Next.js documentation being reachable.
It may require project-specific fixes after codemods complete.
It assumes npm-style commands unless the agent adapts them to the project package manager.

use_cases.title: Upgrade an Application
use_cases.description: Move an existing Next.js app to the latest supported release with codemods and dependency updates.

use_cases.title: Plan a Major Migration
use_cases.description: Review which Next.js major-version guides apply before changing a production codebase.

use_cases.title: Validate Post-Upgrade Health
use_cases.description: Run build and development checks after package updates to find migration errors quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Check Current Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the next-upgrade skill to inspect this project and tell me which Next.js upgrade path applies.

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade to Latest
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the next-upgrade skill to upgrade this project to the latest Next.js version. Ask before running package-manager commands.

prompt_templates.title: Target a Major Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the next-upgrade skill to move this app from its current Next.js version to version 15, including codemods and manual follow-up checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Migration Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the next-upgrade skill to review the completed upgrade, compare it with official guides, and identify remaining manual changes.

output_examples.input: Upgrade this Next.js 14 project to the latest version.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies current Next.js, React, and React DOM versions, fetches the official version guide, proposes codemods, and asks before running installs.

output_examples.input: Tell me what still needs manual work after the codemod.
output: Review async request API changes listed in the official guide.
Check next.config.js for removed or renamed options.
Run the production build and inspect any new type errors.

best_practices: Commit or stash current work before running upgrade commands.
Review official migration guides for every skipped major version.
Run build, tests, and key browser flows before merging the upgrade.
anti_patterns: Running codemods without reading the proposed file changes.
Jumping across major versions without checking intermediate migration notes.
Upgrading production dependencies without a lockfile review.

faq.question: Does this skill run the upgrade automatically?
faq.answer: It gives the agent steps and commands. The agent should ask before running commands that modify the project.

faq.question: Which package managers does it support?
faq.answer: The examples use npm. An agent can adapt commands for pnpm, yarn, or bun when the project uses them.

faq.question: Does it handle App Router migrations?
faq.answer: It points the agent to official Next.js upgrade guides and codemods, including App Router related breaking changes.

faq.question: Can it fix every breaking change?
faq.answer: No. Codemods cover common changes, but project-specific APIs, tests, and configuration may need manual fixes.

faq.question: Why does it need web access?
faq.answer: It fetches current official Next.js migration documentation so guidance matches the target release.

faq.question: Is it safe for large production apps?
faq.answer: Use it with normal release controls, including a clean branch, dependency review, tests, and staged deployment.
